world.title: Trump says uranium will be ‘taken care of’ - as it happened
Pakistan, among countries hit worst by energy crisis, emerges winner in US-Iran ceasefire
world.description: It will likely rankle some in India, but there was no doubt left of the importance of Islamabad, which must be heaving deepest sigh of relief with the two-week window for peace

world.title: Have Trump’s outlandish threats proved successful? We’ll find out in two weeks, or tomorrow
world.description: All we can really say for now is that there appears to be a greater chance of peace today than there was yesterday. And that’s not a bad thing.

world.title: Months in custody for war crime-accused Australian soldier
world.description: Victoria Cross recipient Ben Roberts-Smith will remain in custody before his trial on two counts of murder and three counts of aiding or abetting the same charge.

world.title: Oil slides below $100 after Trump announces two
world.description: Oil fell below $100 per barrel on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump said he had agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran, subject to the immediate and safe reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

world.title: Oil Price Today (April 8): Brent crude oil price crashes 15% as Trump agrees 2-week ceasefire with Iran. What are experts saying?
world.description: Brent Crude oil price: Oil prices plummeted below $100 a barrel following President Trump's announcement of a two-week ceasefire with Iran, contingent on the safe reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. This development, averting a potential escalation, saw Brent crude fall to $94.43 and WTI to $96.82.

world.title: Pope says Trump's threat to destroy Iranian civilization is 'truly unacceptable'
world.description: ROME — Pope Leo XIV said Tuesday that US President Donald Trump’s threat to destroy Iranian civilization was “truly unacceptable” and said any attacks on civilian infrastructure violate international law.

world.title: Oil plunges after US-Iran ceasefire deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
world.description: The price of benchmark Brent crude fell by about 15.9% to $92.30 a barrel, while US-traded oil was almost 16.5% lower at $93.80. Kenya breaking news | Kenya news today |

business.title: Asian shares soar after oil price decline in response to the Iran ceasefire
business.description: Asian shares are surging as oil prices plunged after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire that includes the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

business.title: 2026 Mitsubishi Xforce launched in Malaysia - CKD; 1.5L NA; priced fr RM110k; RM5k rebate until April 12

business.title: Malaysia retail sentiment mixed as fading Raya and Middle East impact weigh
business.description: Malaysia’s retail sector sees mixed outlook as strong consumer confidence is offset by fading post-Raya demand and Middle East tensions.

business.title: Tsakos Energy Navigation: Performing Well In Strong Markets
business.description: Tsakos Energy's fleet is expanding from 62 to over 80 vessels at a time when underlying markets are incredibly strong. Read why TEN stock remains a strong buy.

business.title: Fuel crisis hearing told truck drivers on brink of 'going out of business' without immediate help
business.description: An emergency Fair Work Commission hearing in Sydney begins, with unions and employers in the transport industry saying drivers, employees and businesses are on the verge of going under, calling on the top of the supply chain to share the load.

business.title: Governor Breman sheds light on inflation and policy outlook after RBNZ rate decision
business.description: Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s (RBNZ) new Governor Anna Breman presents the prepared remarks on the policy statement and responds to media questions at the press conference after the April monetary policy announcement.

science.title: Artemis II astronauts capture solar eclipse as they flew around far side of Moon; Mars spotted in frame
science.description: Science News: Astronauts on Nasa’s Artemis II mission have captured a rare image of the Moon eclipsing the Sun, with Mars and two other planets visible in the same .

science.title: Colossal Biosciences: The Moonshot That Became a Business
science.description: By Futurist Thomas Frey In April 2025, three wolf pups were born that shouldn’t exist. Their names were Romulus, Remus, and Khaleesi. They were healthy, they were photographed, and they made the cover of Time magazine. They were also, genetically speaking, something the world hadn’t seen in 13,000 years. The dire wolf — the apex […]

science.title: Internationally renowned researcher named new executive director at The Kids
science.description: A globally recognised leader in maternal and child health has been named The Kids Research Institute Australia’s third Executive Director.

science.title: The Artemis II Crew Has Sent Back Stunning Images From The Far Side of The Moon
science.description: After a historic lunar flyby that carried four astronauts farther from Earth than humanity has ever been, the Artemis II crew has sent home spectacular photos of the far side of the Moon.

science.title: Study: Ice accumulating at moon's poles for at least 1.5 bln years
science.description: Israeli and US scientists have found that ice has been slowly building up at the moon's poles for at least 1.5 billion years, according to a statement from Israel's Weizmann Institute of Science on Tuesday.

science.title: Remarkable discovery inside museum item after 26 years: 'Stopped us in our tracks'
science.description: The creature had even been included in the Guinness Book of Records.

science.title: RNA barcoding maps neural connections with unprecedented resolution
science.description: By tagging neurons with molecular "barcodes," researchers mapped connections among thousands of neurons in the mouse brain with unprecedented speed and resolution.

science.title: Scientists just uncovered the secret behind nature’s “proton highway”
science.description: Scientists have zoomed in on how phosphoric acid moves electrical charges so efficiently in both biology and technology. By freezing a key molecular pair to extremely low temperatures, they found it forms just one stable structure—contrary to predictions. This structure relies on a specific hydrogen-bond network that may be universal in similar systems. The discovery helps explain how protons travel so quickly and could inspire better energy materials.

ai.title: Navigating the Rise of 'Slopaganda': The New Age of AI-Driven Propaganda
ai.description: Slopaganda, a term coined for AI-generated propaganda, is increasingly used to manipulate beliefs and emotions. This phenomenon, blending AI with propagandistic content, dilutes the epistemic environment and erodes public trust. Interventions at individual, industrial, and regulatory levels are necessary to address its impact.

ai.title: AI adopted in higher education, but strategic integration remains elusive
ai.description: Explore the challenges and strategies for effectively integrating AI into higher education beyond mere pilot projects.

ai.title: Anthropic touts AI cybersecurity project with Big Tech partners
ai.description: Anthropic on Tuesday announced an initiative with major technology companies, including Amazon.com, Microsoft and ​Apple

ai.title: Meet Claude Mythos, Anthropic AI so powerful it can hack any software, preview out now
ai.description: Anthropic has a new AI model, called Claude Mythos Preview, which is so good that the AI startup has no plans of making it available to the public anytime soon. Instead, Claude Mythos Preview will be made available to a consortium of over 40 tech companies, such as Apple and Google, that will use the model to fix potential cybersecurity vulnerabilities.

ai.title: Iran war, Oracle layoffs, El Niño worries: What’s keeping the world on edge
ai.description: Global markets face a precarious crossover of exigencies, as West Asia’s war, AI-led job losses and El Niño’s climate risks are causing nervousness around the world.

ai.title: After a 24% crash, IT stocks are rising again. With FII selling slowing, is this the bottom?
ai.description: India's IT sector shows a strong rebound, with the Nifty IT index climbing 8% in April as foreign investors significantly reduced their selling. Valuations are attractive, with many IT stocks trading below historical averages and holding substantial cash. While some strategists see a bottom, others remain cautious about future growth and AI's impact.

ai.title: U.S. envoy to India Gor meets U.S. Commerce secretary Lutnick
ai.description: U.S. envoy Sergio Gor meets Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick to discuss U.S.-India trade, AI collaboration, and market expansion.

ai.title: Anthropic announces 'Project Glasswing' in alliance with tech giants to strengthen global cybersecurity
ai.description: Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, a new initiative that brings together Amazon Web Services, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorganChase, the Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Palo Alto Networks in an effort to 'secure the world's most critical software.' The collaborative effort comes as AI models reach a level of coding capability that allows them to find and exploit software vulnerabilities more effectively than most humans.
